question_text: A tug-of-war rope has 500 N of force pulling from the left and 500 N of force pulling from the right. Describe the motion of the rope.
correct_answer: The rope does not move / The rope remains stationary / The rope stays still
explanation: Since the forces pulling from both sides are equal in size (500 N each) but opposite in direction, the forces are balanced. A balanced net force of zero means the rope will not accelerate or move; it will stay in the same position.
